step_by_step_thinking: I agree with the consensus that Glycopyrrolate bromide (Option A) should be selected. One additional point worth noting is the extensive research indicating that glycopyrrolate bromide specifically targets peripheral muscarinic receptors without significant activation of central nervous system receptors, further corroborating its minimal interaction with the blood-brain barrier. This precise mechanism also contributes to why it poses fewer risks concerning teratogenicity in pregnant women. Thus, my agreement stands firm with Option A being the correct choice among the given options. Final
